instruction: Using Terminal, copy the entire directory hierarchy from ~/{SRC} to ~/{DST}, preserving its subfolder structure.
source: adapted:osworld:4783cc41-c03c-4e1b-89b4-50658f642bd5 mode=port; OSWorld os 'copy directory hierarchy from $sourceDir to $targetDir' is a generic recursive-copy task; maps verbatim to Terminal/exec (cp -R) with a Tier-A exec getter that diffs the two trees

args: a=$(cd "$HOME/{SRC}" 2>/dev/null && find . | sort); b=$(cd "$HOME/{DST}" 2>/dev/null && find . | sort); [ -n "$a" ] && [ "$a" = "$b" ] && echo match || echo differ
